title: rubus hirtus var. rudericola
intro: Rubus hirtus var. rudericola, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, specifically a variant of the Rubus hirtus species. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. rudericola include reducing inflammation, aiding in wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. The plant is often prepared as a tea, extract, or tincture, and can be used in traditional herbal remedies to leverage its medicinal properties.
